name: RefRef
version: No stable releases; current repo active with alpha/breaking-change warnings
version_status: CONFIRMED project state / exact stable version MISSING
what: Open-source referral platform for structured referral programs and community-driven acquisition flows.
solves: Referral tracking, incentives, codes/links, and program analytics for partner or customer referral motion.
users: Growth teams and communities running formal referral programs.
fit: Potentially useful for Hylono partner/referral programs, but not a core infrastructure or customer-operations need on day one.
runtime: Web application with database, referral logic, admin interfaces, and integrations.
components: Program definitions, participants, referral tracking, rewards logic, and admin/reporting surfaces.
deploy: Self-host docs exist, but the project signals alpha/breaking changes; production use requires caution.
deps: Database, mail/notification tooling, tracking links/events, and reward process design.
auth: Local users/admins; verify current auth/SSO surfaces before assuming enterprise integration.
apis: Referral-program APIs and web surfaces; verify maturity before integration.
security: Contains participant/contact and reward data. Program abuse prevention also matters.
setup_path: Do not put into production without testing maturity and workflow completeness.
env: DB settings, app URL, mail, secrets, and any reward/integration config.
persistence: Back up program DB and audit referral events if used.
operations: Program abuse handling, reward reconciliation, and upstream breaking changes are the main risks.
failure_points: Assuming alpha software is ready for customer-facing programs without additional hardening.
integration: Could tie to Hylono referral or ambassador programs later via Next.js, n8n, CRM, and notifications.
verify: Confirm whether Hylono actually needs a referral platform and whether the project maturity is acceptable.
gdpr: Participant and referral-contact data require standard privacy controls.
overlap: Could be replaced by lighter n8n + CRM + coupon tooling for early programs.
codex_read_first: Read project maturity notes before considering implementation.
codex_best_path: Treat as experimental unless a real referral program exists and product maturity is validated.
codex_never_assume: Never assume alpha/open-source referral tooling is production-ready for customer trust-sensitive programs.

assets.name: Self-host docs
assets.notes: Deployment and project docs live in repo.
